description: Semantically similar code pairs for testing duplicate/reuse detection in the RAG system.

pairs.description: EmailValidator.validate and URLValidator.validate both use compiled regex via re.match
pairs.notes: Both compile a pattern at module level, call pattern.match(value), and return ValidationResult.fail on mismatch.
Both lowercase and strip email. Legacy version also removes spaces (slightly different behaviour).
Both strip non-digit characters. normalize_phone uses re.sub (faster); _legacy iterates char by char.
Both produce URL-safe slugs. slugify handles unicode normalization (NFKD); _legacy_to_slug does not.

pairs.description: ListValidator item-level error collection pattern mirrors DictValidator's pattern
pairs.notes: Both iterate children, call item_validator, accumulate errors list, and return ValidationResult(is_valid=False, errors=...) on failure.

pairs.description: FutureDateValidator and PastDateValidator share identical date-parsing logic
pairs.notes: Both branches parse date from datetime/date/str using the same 3-branch isinstance check before comparing to reference date.

pairs.description: PositiveValidator and NegativeValidator are near-symmetric implementations
pairs.notes: Same structure: type check, allow_zero branch, comparison, return ok/fail.

pairs.description: AllValidator and ChainValidator both iterate validators but differ in what value they pass forward
pairs.notes: Key difference: AllValidator collects all errors; ChainValidator short-circuits and pipes coerced value.

pairs.description: to_upper and to_lower in converters.py are mirror functions
ValidationCache.get and ValidationCache.set both call _make_key and use the _lock context manager
pairs.notes: Both call self._make_key then operate under self._lock. Candidate for a shared internal helper.
